article_meta.title: Polling analyst Patrick Allocco told Newsmax on Monday that despite wide disparities in early surveys on U.S

raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Bias by Omission","biasedText":"The article interviews only one polling analyst who downplays the impact of the strikes on Republican voters and frames the divergence in polls as a methodological issue.","explanation":"The article presents only one source — a single polling analyst — who consistently minimizes political risk for Trump and Republicans. No opposing analysts, critics of the strikes, or voices arguing the polls reflect genuine public disapproval are included anywhere in the piece."},{"biasType":"Slant","biasedText":"A Reuters survey released Monday found only 27% of American adults approved of the strikes. An InsiderAdvantage poll, released Sunday, showed 54% approval.","explanation":"The author presents raw polling data but then immediately frames the divergence through Allocco's lens — that it's a methodological/wording issue — without presenting any alternative interpretation. The 27% approval figure and CNN's finding that 23% of Republicans disapproved are structurally de-emphasized, mentioned briefly before the article returns to Allocco's reassuring analysis."},{"biasType":"Sensationalism and Emotionalism","biasedText":"NEWSMAX is the fastest-growing cable news channel in America with more than 30 million people watching!","explanation":"The promotional material embedded at the end of the article uses sensationalized, hyperbolic language to advertise the outlet, blurring the line between news content and self-promotion."}]}

detections.biasedText: The article interviews only one polling analyst who downplays the impact of the strikes on Republican voters and frames the divergence in polls as a methodological issue.
detections.explanation: The article presents only one source — a single polling analyst — who consistently minimizes political risk for Trump and Republicans. No opposing analysts, critics of the strikes, or voices arguing the polls reflect genuine public disapproval are included anywhere in the piece.

detections.biasedText: A Reuters survey released Monday found only 27% of American adults approved of the strikes. An InsiderAdvantage poll, released Sunday, showed 54% approval.
detections.explanation: The author presents raw polling data but then immediately frames the divergence through Allocco's lens — that it's a methodological/wording issue — without presenting any alternative interpretation. The 27% approval figure and CNN's finding that 23% of Republicans disapproved are structurally de-emphasized, mentioned briefly before the article returns to Allocco's reassuring analysis.

detections.biasedText: NEWSMAX is the fastest-growing cable news channel in America with more than 30 million people watching!
detections.explanation: The promotional material embedded at the end of the article uses sensationalized, hyperbolic language to advertise the outlet, blurring the line between news content and self-promotion.
